data.title: Heard on the Street Roundup: When Buffett Went Bottom-Fishing — WSJ
data.description:
By Heard Editors

What Happened in Markets Today

News that the U.S. could become a shareholder in Intel continues to boost the troubled chip maker's stock. A deal could help advance Trump's America-first manufacturing agenda-and mark yet another intervention by him into the private sector. Intel shares had their best week since 1975, jumping more than 7% Thursday after news of the deal talks emerged, and another 2.9% on Friday.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: BioSig Technologies, Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
BioSig Technologies, Inc., a company specializing in advanced signal processing solutions for the medical industry, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance and key business activities, highlighting the challenges and strategic initiatives undertaken during this period.

Financial Highlights

Revenue: BioSig Technologies reported no revenue for both the three and six months ended June 30, 2025, reflecting its early commercialization stage.

Loss from operations: The company incurred a loss from operations of $20.365 million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, and $23.350 million for the six months ended June 30, 2025, indicating significant operating expenses relative to revenue.

Net loss: BioSig Technologies reported a net loss of $20.368 million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, and $23.169 million for the six months ended June 30, 2025, highlighting ongoing challenges in achieving profitability.

Net loss per common share, basic and diluted: The net loss per common share was $(0.74) for the three months ended June 30, 2025, and $(0.95) for the six months ended June 30, 2025, reflecting the impact of net losses on shareholder value.

Business Highlights

Business Acquisition: On May 28, 2025, BioSig Technologies completed the acquisition of Streamex Exchange Corporation, a software development company specializing in digital tools for commodity trading and finance. This acquisition is expected to enhance BioSig's product offerings and accelerate blockchain integration.

Geographical Performance: As of June 30, 2025, a significant portion of BioSig's assets, approximately 97.16%, were located in Canada, primarily related to the Streamex business. These assets consist mainly of goodwill and intangible assets.

New Product Launches: The acquisition of Streamex is anticipated to expand BioSig's capabilities in digital commodity trading and blockchain-based financial technologies, potentially leading to new product offerings in these areas.

Future Outlook: The company is focused on enhancing shareholder value through the integration of Streamex's technology and expertise. BioSig plans to seek shareholder approval to facilitate the full conversion of exchangeable shares issued in the acquisition, which could significantly impact its capital structure and operational strategy.

Segment Reporting: BioSig operates as a single reportable segment, managing its operations as one integrated business without preparing or reviewing discrete financial information for individual business units or subsidiaries.

SEC Filing: BioSig Technologies, Inc. [ BSGM ] - 10-Q - Aug. 15, 2025

data.title: Moatable Reports Second Quarter 2025 Financial Results
data.description:
PHOENIX, Aug. 15, 2025 /PRNewswire/ — Moatable, Inc. ("Moatable" or the "Company"), a leading US-based SaaS company, today reported its second quarter 2025 financial results.

Second Quarter 2025 Financial Highlights

  • Revenue increased 26% over Q2 2024 to $19.3 million in Q2 2025; Revenue for the six months ended June 30, 2025, increased 27%, to $37.3 million, compared to the same period last year.
  • Gross profit increased 21% over Q2 2024 to $14.3 million; Gross profit for the six months ended June 30, 2025, increased 23% to $27.7 million, compared to the same period last year.
  • Gain (loss) from operations improved 150% from a loss of $0.8 million in Q2 2024 to a gain of $0.4 million in Q2 2025; loss from operations for the six months ended June 30, 2025, improved 77% to $0.5 million compared to $2.2 million in the same period last year.
  • Adjusted EBITDA* improved 100% from a profit of $0.5 million in Q2 2024 to a profit of $1.0 million in Q2 2025; adjusted EBITDA for the six months ended June 30, 2025, improved 20% to a profit of $0.6 million compared to a profit of $0.5 million in the same period last year.
  • Total cash & cash equivalents and restricted cash of $22.1 million as of the end of Q2 2025 compared to $31.9 million as of the end of 2024.

"We are very pleased with the continued steady revenue growth over the past fourteen quarters and are particularly encouraged by our profitability. We generated a net income of $0.4 million in the second quarter of 2025, compared to a net loss of $0.5 million in the same quarter last year. Our Adjusted EBITDA of $1.0 million profit in Q2 2025 shows significant improvement over that of $0.5 million in the same quarter of 2024, as we continue to rationalize our cost structure and maintain our path to profitability," said Scott Stone, the chief financial officer of Moatable.

* Adjusted EBITDA is a non-GAAP measure. We define adjusted EBITDA as loss from operations excluding share-based compensation expenses, depreciation and amortization expenses, impairment of intangibles, and certain other non-recurring expenses. See the table "Reconciliation of Non-GAAP Financial Measure to the Comparable GAAP Financial Measure" below for details.

About Moatable Inc.

Moatable, Inc. (OTC Pink: MTBLY) operates two US-based SaaS businesses including Lofty and Trucker Path. Moatable's American depositary shares, each of which currently represents forty-five Class A ordinary shares, trade on OTC Pink open market under the symbol "MTBLY". For more news and information on Moatable, please visit Moatable.com.

Forward-Looking Statements

This announcement contains forward-looking statements. These statements are made under the "safe harbor" provisions of the U.S. Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995. These forward-looking statements can be identified by terminology such as "will," "expects," "anticipates," "future," "intends," "plans," "believes," "estimates" and similar statements. Statements that are not historical facts, including statements about Moatable's beliefs and expectations, including statements on making investments and operating businesses that generate long-term returns for investors, and expectations for future growth and innovation are forward-looking statements. Forward-looking statements involve inherent risks and uncertainties. A number of factors could cause actual results to differ materially from those contained in any forward-looking statement, including but not limited to the following: Moatable's goals and strategies; Moatable's future business development, financial condition and results of operations; Moatable's expectations regarding demand for and market acceptance of its services; Moatable's plans to enhance user experience, infrastructure and service offerings. Further information regarding these and other risks is included in our recent annual and quarterly reports on Form 10-K and Form 10-Q and other documents filed with the SEC. All information provided in this press release is as of the date of this press release, and Moatable does not undertake any obligation to update any forward-looking statement, except as required under applicable law.

Non-GAAP Financial Information

This press release includes certain financial measures that are not presented in accordance with U.S. generally accepted accounting principles ("GAAP"), including Adjusted EBITDA. We define Adjusted EBITDA as loss from operations excluding equity-based compensation, depreciation and amortization, impairment of intangibles, and certain other non-recurring expenses. See "Reconciliation of Non-GAAP Financial Measure to the Comparable GAAP Financial Measure" below.

We believe that these non-GAAP financial measures are provided to enhance the reader's understanding of our past financial performance and our prospects for the future. Our management team uses these non-GAAP financial measures in assessing the Company's performance, as well as in planning and forecasting future periods. The non-GAAP financial information is presented for supplemental informational purposes only and should not be considered a substitute for financial information presented in accordance with GAAP, and may be different from similarly titled non-GAAP measures used by other companies.

data.title: True North Commercial REIT Announces August 2025 Distribution
data.description:
/NOT FOR DISTRIBUTION IN THE U.S. OR OVER U.S. NEWSWIRES/

TORONTO, Aug. 15, 2025 /CNW/ - True North Commercial Real Estate Investment Trust (the "REIT") today announced its August 2025 monthly cash distribution in the amount of $0.0575 per trust unit ("Unit"), payable on September 15, 2025 to holders of Units of record at August 31, 2025.

About the REIT

The REIT is an unincorporated, open-ended real estate investment trust established under the laws of the Province of Ontario. The REIT currently owns and operates a portfolio of 38 properties consisting of approximately 4.5 million square feet in urban and select strategic secondary markets across Canada. The REIT is focused on growing its portfolio principally through acquisitions across Canada and such other jurisdictions where opportunities exist.

For more information regarding the REIT, please visit www.sedarplus.ca or the REIT's website at www.truenorthreit.com.

SOURCE True North Commercial Real Estate Investment Trust

data.title: Dow Industrials Hit First Intraday High of 2025, Powered by UnitedHealth Shares — WSJ
data.description:
By Vicky Ge Huang

A major rally in UnitedHealth Group stock propelled the Dow Jones Industrial Average to its first intraday record of 2025.

UnitedHealth, the Dow's biggest laggard since early April, jumped 12% on Friday after a filing showed that Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway took a roughly $1.6 billion position in the healthcare company during the second quarter. UnitedHealth stock remains down 40% for the year.

Friday's gains faded in afternoon trading, leaving the Dow Industrials just short of its closing record. Nevertheless, investors view its climb as a sign of the rally's expanding breadth and depth, considering that a handful of megacap tech stocks, known as the Magnificent Seven, have driven most of the S&P 500's returns in recent years.

"There's a lot of market optimism and I am happy to see the Dow moving," said David Bahnsen, chief investment officer of The Bahnsen Group. "Because if all you have is the S&P and Nasdaq making a new high, it probably means that we're just getting more and more top-heavy with those tech names. But when the Dow is doing it, that's not the explanation."

The Dow climbed 35 points, or just shy of 0.1%, after earlier touching 45203.52, an all-time high. The S&P 500 fell 0.3%, while the tech-focused Nasdaq composite lost 0.4%.

The Dow last closed at an all-time high in December. The S&P 500 and Nasdaq clinched their 18th and 20th record closes of 2025 this week, respectively.

The delay is a consequence of the Dow's price-weighted methodology, which gives its high-price underperformers this year, including UnitedHealth and Salesforce, a disproportionately large impact on its performance.

The Dow's portfolio of 30 stocks is also heavily weighted toward industrial and financial companies such as Boeing and Goldman Sachs, giving it less exposure to technology and other high-growth companies that have driven the broader market rally this year. Goldman has been the benchmark's biggest contributor to this year's gains, rising 28%.

All three major indexes ended the week higher. The Dow led the gains with a 1.7% increase. The S&P 500 advanced 0.9%, and the Nasdaq added 0.8%.

The yield on the 10-year Treasury note, a key indicator of borrowing costs across the economy, inched higher to 4.325%. Yields rise as prices fall.

On Friday, the Commerce Department said retail sales ticked up 0.5% in July from June, suggesting that consumer spending remains resilient despite trade and economic uncertainty. However, the latest readout from the University of Michigan's long-running sentiment survey shows that consumers' mood has soured due to expectations of more inflation ahead.

Earlier this week, a mild government report on consumer prices boosted hopes that the Federal Reserve would cut interest rates in September. But on Thursday, hotter-than-expected wholesale inflation data renewed doubts about the rate outlook.

Interest-rate futures signaled by Friday afternoon that traders see a September cut as an 87% likelihood, down from 92% on Thursday, according to CME Group data.

"Economic growth is moderating and that should continue to support the idea that we're moving past the need for restrictive policy," said Trevor Slaven, global head of asset allocation at Barings. "I do expect that the Fed is going to cut at least twice this year."

Chip stocks declined after President Trump said he could announce tariffs on semiconductor imports next week, indicating that the tariffs would start at lower levels and then rise over time.

Applied Materials plummeted 14% after the semiconductor-equipment maker forecast lower profit and revenue this quarter. Rival KLA and Lam Research also fell 8.4% and 7.3%, respectively.

Shares of Intel gained 2.9% after reports that the chip maker and the Trump administration are discussing the government potentially taking a stake in the company.

Overseas, Hong Kong's benchmark dropped about 1% after data showed China's economy slowed in July. The Stoxx Europe 600 inched lower by less than 0.1% ahead of President Trump's face-to-face meeting with Russian President Vladimir Putin to discuss prospects for ending the Ukraine war.

Write to Vicky Ge Huang at vicky.huang@wsj.com

data.title: BRINKER INTERNATIONAL, INC SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Brinker International, Inc., the parent company of popular restaurant brands Chili’s Grill & Bar and Maggiano’s Little Italy, has released its annual 10-K report for the fiscal year 2025. The report highlights significant financial growth, strategic initiatives aimed at enhancing financial flexibility, and the challenges the company faces in the competitive restaurant industry.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenues: $5,384.2 million, reflecting a 21.9% increase from the previous fiscal year, driven by higher traffic, favorable menu item mix, and menu pricing.
  • Operating Income: $512.0 million, representing a 9.5% margin of Total Revenues, up from 5.2% in the prior year, indicating improved operational efficiency.
  • Net Income: $383.1 million, a significant increase from $155.3 million in the previous year, attributed to higher revenues and effective cost management.
  • Basic Net Income Per Share: $8.60, compared to $3.49 in the prior year, reflecting the company's strong financial performance.
  • Diluted Net Income Per Share: $8.32, up from $3.40 in the previous year, highlighting the company's improved profitability.

Business Highlights

  • Restaurant Brands: Brinker International, Inc. operates two main restaurant brands: Chili’s Grill & Bar and Maggiano’s Little Italy. Chili’s is a leader in the casual dining industry with a global presence, while Maggiano’s offers Italian-American cuisine with a focus on special occasions and large parties.
  • Geographical Performance: Chili’s has a global presence with restaurants in the United States, 27 other countries, and two U.S. territories. The international expansion is driven by development agreements with franchise partners, with 30 new international locations opened in fiscal 2025.
  • Sales Units: In fiscal 2025, the average annual net sales per Company-owned Chili’s restaurant was $4.5 million, and the average revenue per meal was approximately $21.90 per guest. For Maggiano’s, the average annual sales per restaurant was $9.9 million, with an average revenue per meal of approximately $39.06 per guest.
  • New Restaurant Openings: During fiscal 2025, Brinker opened new Company-owned restaurants in strategically desirable markets, focusing on both major metropolitan areas and smaller market areas. The company plans to open 5 new Chili’s domestic locations in fiscal 2026.
  • Franchise Development: In fiscal 2025, 34 franchise-operated restaurants were opened, including 3 domestic and 30 international Chili’s locations. The company projects 26-32 new franchise openings in fiscal 2026.
  • Operational Strategy: Chili’s strategy focuses on simplifying the menu to core items like burgers and fajitas, enhancing digital experiences, and improving hospitality. Maggiano’s emphasizes warm hospitality and catering to special occasions, with a focus on banquet facilities and carry-out options.
  • Future Outlook: Brinker plans to continue expanding its restaurant brands domestically and internationally, with a focus on markets that improve competitive position and profitability. The company also aims to enhance digital and off-premise dining experiences to meet evolving guest preferences.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Financial Flexibility: Brinker International, Inc. has focused on enhancing its financial flexibility by amending its revolving credit facility, increasing the capacity from $900 million to $1 billion, which matures on May 1, 2030. This strategic move is aimed at supporting the company's liquidity and capital expenditure needs.
  • Debt Management: The company has been actively managing its debt profile by repaying $350 million of 5.00% senior notes using borrowings under the revolving credit facility.
  • Capital Management: The company has been proactive in capital management through its share repurchase program, repurchasing 1.0 million shares for $76 million in fiscal 2025. The Board of Directors authorized an additional $400 million under the share repurchase program, increasing the total available authority to $507 million. No dividends were declared in fiscal 2025 or 2024, reflecting a focus on share repurchases and debt management.
  • Future Outlook: Brinker International plans to maintain a strong focus on cash flow generation and financial flexibility to navigate the unpredictable macroeconomic environment, including commodity and labor inflation and supply chain disruptions. The company believes its current cash position, coupled with cash generated from operations and the available credit facility, will be sufficient to meet its capital expenditure and working capital needs for at least the next twelve months. The company will continue to assess the macro environment and adjust its capital allocation strategy, including share repurchases, based on market conditions and trends.

Challenges and Risks

  • Operational Risks: Brinker International faces challenges in maintaining brand consistency and managing franchise operations, especially in international markets where cultural and regulatory differences may impact product specifications and operations.
  • Strategic and Operational Risks: The company faces risks including the inability to successfully design and execute business strategies, changes in consumer preferences, and food safety incidents. The reliance on third-party delivery providers poses risks related to service quality and availability. Additionally, the company faces challenges in recruiting and retaining high-quality management and team members due to a competitive labor market.
  • Macroeconomic and Industry Risks: Economic conditions, such as inflation and fluctuations in energy costs, can negatively impact consumer discretionary spending and the company’s financial performance. Supply chain disruptions and shortages in food and other products may increase costs or reduce revenues.
  • Information and Technology-Related Risks: Cybersecurity threats and the protection of confidential information are critical concerns. The company’s technology systems are essential for operations, and any failure or breach could significantly impact reputation and financial condition.
  • Financial Risks: Potential downgrades in credit ratings could impact the company’s ability to access capital. Changes in the market price of common stock or impairment of goodwill could adversely affect financial position and results of operations.
  • Legal and Regulatory Risks: Compliance with various federal, state, and local laws and regulations, including employment and labor laws, is essential. Changes in tax laws and unanticipated tax liabilities could also adversely affect profitability.
  • Management Strategies: The company’s operating results have been impacted by geopolitical and macroeconomic events, leading to inflation in wages and food costs. Management is focused on strategies to grow sales, increase profits, and engage team members. The company is investing in technology and off-premise options to enhance the guest experience and drive sales.
  • Market Risks: The company is exposed to market risks, including fluctuations in commodity prices and interest rates. These risks could impact the cost of operations and financial results. The company’s ability to manage these risks is critical to maintaining profitability and competitive advantage.

SEC Filing: BRINKER INTERNATIONAL, INC [ EAT ] - 10-K - Aug. 15, 2025

data.title: World equities flat, crude oil prices fall as Trump, Putin hold high-stakes talks
data.description:
Key points:
  • Dow edges higher after hitting record intra-day high
  • Benchmark S&P 500 and Nasdaq fall
  • Crude oil prices shed $1 as Trump-Putin meeting begins
  • US dollar weakens, gold prices rise
  • US Treasury bond prices rises across board

Global stocks were flat but still traded near record highs on Friday as U.S. President Donald Trump and his Russian counterpart Vladimir Putin held high-stakes talks in Alaska over Ukraine.

U.S. Treasury bond prices fell across the board with markets anticipating a Federal Reserve interest rate cut.

The Dow hit an intra-day record high during the session, becoming the last of Wall Street's main indexes to climb to a new peak this week. The benchmark S&P 500 and the Nasdaq dropped, dragged down mainly by technology, financials, industrials and utilities stocks. The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 0.08%, the S&P 500 fell 0.29% and the Nasdaq Composite fell 0.40%.

"This market continues to move higher and the story is just earnings and margins," said Talley Leger, chief market strategist at The Wealth Consulting Group in New Jersey.

"The inflation numbers that we saw this week were mostly services and in a services-based economy like ours, this is good for profit margins."

Data showed that U.S. retail sales increased solidly in July, rising 0.5% from the prior month, after an unexpected spike in producer price data on Thursday renewed inflation concerns and pared market expectations for Federal Reserve rate cuts this year.

European shares touched a near five-month high before pulling back, as investors drew encouragement from a largely positive earnings season. The pan-European STOXX 600 index finished flat at 0.06%.

The MSCI All Country World Index EURONEXT:IACWI consolidated recent gains. It was last flat at 951.70, just shy of the record level of 954.21 set on Wednesday.

Trump and Putin met face to face in Alaska in a high-stakes meeting that could determine whether a ceasefire can be reached in the deadliest war in Europe since World War Two.

Trump has said a second summit involving Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskiy could follow if the talks with Putin go well.

Details and the longevity of any agreement will be key, and for now investors are on standby. Ukraine's government bonds - key indicators of the mood - have largely stalled in recent days at a still-distressed 55 cents on the dollar.

"There's still a small degree of risk premium in European markets because of the war. Any type of resolution will ultimately pare that back," said Shaniel Ramjee, co-head of multi-asset at Pictet Asset Management, adding that oil and other commodity prices could also react.

"But I think that the market has learnt not to expect too much from these negotiations. Ultimately, Zelenskiy and the Europeans are not invited. They will need to be involved in any final negotiation," Ramjee added.

The two-year note (US2YT=RR) yield, which typically moves in step with interest rate expectations for the Fed, rose 1.1 basis points to 3.751%, paring earlier losses. The yield on benchmark U.S. 10-year notes rose 2.7 basis points to 4.32%.

In currency markets, the dollar weakened 0.38% to 146.72 against the Japanese yen and was down 0.15% at 0.806 against the Swiss franc . The euro > was up 0.48% at $1.1702.

The dollar index , which tracks the greenback against a basket of six major currencies, was last trading down 0.34% at 97.85.

Japanese GDP data released on Friday showed the economy expanding by an annualised 1.0% in the April-to-June quarter, beating analyst estimates.

Brent crude fell 1.5% to settle at $66.85 per barrel. U.S. crude fell 1.8% to settle at $62.80.

Spot gold rose 0.09% to $3,338.65 an ounce. U.S. gold futures (GCc1) settled almost flat at $3,382.60.

Cryptocurrency markets stabilised after bitcoin touched a record $124,480.82 on Thursday. It was down 0.78% at $117,033.52.

data.title: Supercom Ltd reports results for the quarter ended June 30 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Supercom Ltd reported quarterly adjusted earnings of 64 cents​​ per share for the quarter ended June 30, lower than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of $1.20. The mean expectation of two analysts for the quarter was for earnings of 36 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from 3 cents to 68 cents per share.

  • Revenue fell 5.4% to $7.14 million from a year ago; analysts expected $6.46 million.

  • Supercom Ltd's reported EPS for the quarter was 64 cents​.

  • The company reported quarterly net income of $1.1 million.

  • Supercom Ltd shares had fallen by 6.3% this quarter and gained 101.8% so far this year.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts was unchanged in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, there have been no earnings estimate revisions by analysts covering the company.

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 2 "strong buy" or "buy," no "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell." The average consensus recommendation for the software peer group is also "buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Supercom Ltd is $15.00, about 35.2% above its last closing price of $9.73

data.title: Killam Apartment REIT Announces August 2025 Distribution
data.description:
HALIFAX, NS, Aug. 15, 2025 /CNW/ - Killam Apartment REIT is pleased to announce its August 2025 monthly distribution. The distribution of $0.06 per unit will be paid on September 15, 2025, to unitholders of record on August 29, 2025.

Killam Apartment REIT offers a distribution reinvestment plan (the "DRIP"). Eligible unitholders may reinvest their cash distributions, on each distribution payment date, in additional units. Participating unitholders will receive an additional distribution of units representing 3% of the amount of the distribution reinvested pursuant to the DRIP.

About Killam Apartment REIT

Killam Apartment REIT, based in Halifax, Nova Scotia, is one of Canada's largest residential real estate investment trusts, owning, operating, managing and developing a $5.5 billion portfolio of apartments and manufactured home communities. Killam's strategy to enhance value and profitability focuses on three priorities: 1) increasing earnings from existing operations, 2) expanding the portfolio and diversifying geographically through accretive acquisitions which target newer properties and through the disposition of non-core assets, and 3) developing high-quality properties in its core markets.

data.title: Dow Jones Top Markets Headlines at 5 PM ET: Retail Sales Notch Another Strong Month. Wall Street Was Depending on It. | Americans ...
data.description:
Retail Sales Notch Another Strong Month. Wall Street Was Depending on It.

Consumer spending ticked up in July in line with economists' expectations, suggesting American households remain resilient.

----

Americans more worried about inflation and unemployment after new trade deals leave tariffs at highest level in decades

Fresh worries about inflation soured Americans on the economy in early August, underscoring lingering anxiety about the highest U.S. tariffs in decades and a further rise in unemployment.

----

Week Ahead for FX, Bonds: Fed's Powell Comments at Jackson Hole in Focus

Comments from U.S. Federal Reserve President Jerome Powell at the Jackson Hole Symposium, alongside minutes to the Fed's most recent meeting, will be closely watched as investors increasingly anticipate that interest-rate cuts will resume next month.

----

U.S. Stocks End Mixed, Dow Boosted by UnitedHealth

U.S. stocks were ended mixed, with the Dow edging higher after a vote of confidence from Warren Buffett in struggling health insurer UnitedHealth.

----

Trump, Putin Head to High-Stakes Alaska Summit With Clashing Objectives

The two leaders are set to meet in person for the first time since the start of Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine.

----

Why Haven't Tariffs Boosted Inflation? This Theory Is Gaining Traction

New research suggests the actual tariff rates are well below what economists have suspected.

----

China's Economy Shows Signs of Slowing, Raising Pressure on Beijing

The Chinese economy lost some momentum in July, backing the case for policymakers to snap out of wait-and-see mode and stimulate growth.

----

Japan's Economy Records Modest Growth Despite Trade Uncertainty

Japan's latest economic data has calmed recession fears, showing a fifth straight quarter of growth despite the impact of U.S. tariffs.

----

Swiss Economy Slows Sharply as Tariffs Weigh

Switzerland's economic growth slowed sharply in the second quarter as U.S. tariff frontrunning seen earlier in the year unwound.

----

M&A is back and corporate earnings are up. Do America's biggest companies need rate cuts?

America's largest companies aren't making a slam-dunk case for the Federal Reserve to quickly slash interest rates.

----

Canada PM Carney Aims to Resolve Canola Row With China

Officials will work on a support package for canola growers while trying to resolve the trade row with their Chinese counterparts over the oilseed, said Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney.

Even though Warren Buffett seems to think the value of UnitedHealth stock is about right, that doesn't mean investors should ignore the long list of unresolved issues facing the company.

----

Intel's Move Toward Nationalization Won't Work-at Least for the Long Haul

Federal support could get the troubled chip maker over some hurdles, but risks great harm to the U.S. tech sector.

----

Joby Takes Key Step Toward Commercial Service

----

Ukraine's Kyivstar Shares Fall on Nasdaq Listing Debut

----

Fast-Casual Chains Struggle as Diners Ditch Pricey Bowls for Cheaper Eats

Opendoor CEO Resigns. The Meme Stock Is Getting New Leadership.

Rivian Says It Faces $100 Million Hole After Relaxation of Fuel Economy Rules

The admission is a sign of how changes to automotive policy under the Trump administration are starting to hurt the electric-vehicle industry.

Lyft Co-Founders to Exit Board, Wrapping Two-Year Succession Plan

Cognition Cinches About $500 Million to Advance AI Code-Generation Business

Pandora Reiterates Full-Year Outlook But Flags Increasing Headwinds

Why Cheap Airline Seats Are Getting Harder to Find

How Urban Outfitters' Nuuly Stole the Show From Rent the Runway

Starbucks' CEO Scored in His First Year. Now Comes the Hard Part.

data.title: CopAur Minerals Sells Bolo Project Interest, Strengthening Focus on Flagship Kinsley Mountain
data.description:
Vancouver, British Columbia--(Newsfile Corp. - August 15, 2025) - CopAur Minerals Inc.  (the "Company" or "CopAur") announces that it has sold its minority interest in the Bolo Gold Project (the "Property") to Allegiant Gold Ltd. ("Allegiant").

This transaction is consistent with the Company's strategic decision to focus its attention and resources on advancing its 100%-owned, past-producing Kinsley Mountain Oxide Gold Project in Nevada.

Under the terms of the agreement, CopAur will receive cash consideration and the forgiveness of certain debt obligations owed to Allegiant by CopAur.

About CopAur

CopAur is an exploration company focused on developing projects within the emerging, mineral-rich mining regions of Nevada. The Company is backed by a dynamic and experienced team of resource professionals advancing its projects in Nevada with the flagship project being Kinsley Mountain Gold Project, a Carlin-style project located 90 kilometres south of the Long Canyon mine (currently in production under the Newmont/Barrick joint venture, Nevada Gold Mines).

On Behalf Of The Board Of CopAur Minerals Inc.Conrad Swanson, Chairman

For more information, please contact ir@copaur.com

data.title: Moatable, Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Moatable, Inc., a leading provider of SaaS solutions, has released its latest Form 10-Q report, showcasing significant improvements in both financial and operational performance. The company's diverse portfolio, including its flagship platforms Lofty and Trucker Path, has driven substantial growth and positioned Moatable, Inc. for continued success in the SaaS industry.

Financial Highlights

Moatable, Inc. reported robust financial results for the latest quarter, reflecting the company's strategic focus on expanding its SaaS offerings and improving operational efficiency. Key financial metrics include:

  • Total Revenues: $19.276 million, reflecting an increase from the previous period driven by growth in SaaS revenue.
  • Gross Profit: $14.293 million, indicating improved efficiency in cost management.
  • (Loss) Income from operations: $0.446 million, showing a positive turnaround from a loss in the previous period.
  • Net (loss) income: $0.435 million, demonstrating a recovery from a net loss in the prior period.
  • Net (loss) income per share attributable to Moatable, Inc. shareholders - Basic: $0.0006, highlighting a return to profitability on a per-share basis.
  • Net (loss) income per share attributable to Moatable, Inc. shareholders - Diluted: $0.0006, consistent with basic earnings per share, reflecting no dilution effect.

Business Highlights

Moatable, Inc. continues to make significant strides in its operational performance, driven by its innovative SaaS platforms and strategic acquisitions. Key business highlights include:

  • Revenue Segments: Moatable, Inc. operates two main SaaS platforms, Lofty and Trucker Path. Lofty focuses on real estate sales acceleration and client lifecycle management, while Trucker Path provides a driver-centric online transportation management platform. For the three months ended June 30, 2025, Lofty generated $9,895 in revenue, and Trucker Path generated $9,342.
  • Geographical Performance: The majority of Moatable, Inc.'s revenue is generated from the U.S. market, which remains the primary focus for its SaaS businesses. The company continues to leverage its strong presence in the U.S. to drive growth in its core segments.
  • New Product Launches: During the reporting period, Moatable, Inc. acquired Truckers Best Insurance LLC to enter the insurance brokerage business, expanding its service offerings within the Trucker Path segment.
  • Future Outlook: Moatable, Inc. is focused on leveraging synergies from its recent acquisition of Truckers Best Insurance LLC to enhance its service offerings and drive future revenue growth. The company is also committed to expanding its SaaS platforms and exploring new market opportunities to sustain its growth trajectory.

SEC Filing: Moatable, Inc. [ MTBLY ] - 10-Q - Aug. 15, 2025

data.title: GRAPHJET TECHNOLOGY SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Graphjet Technology, a company specializing in the production of high-quality graphene and graphite, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2024. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance and key business activities during the reporting period. Despite not generating revenue, Graphjet has made significant strides in its business operations, including a notable merger and advancements in production technology.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenues: $0 million. The company did not generate any revenue during the reporting period.
  • Gross Profit: $0 million. With no revenue, the gross profit remains zero.
  • Loss from operations: $(1.21) million. Reflects a decrease in operating expenses compared to the previous year.
  • Net loss: $(1.27) million. The company continues to incur losses as it has not yet commenced sales of its products.
  • Loss per share ordinary share - Basic: $(0.01). Reflects the net loss distributed over the weighted average number of shares.

Business Highlights

  • Business Combination: On March 14, 2024, Graphjet Technology completed a merger with Energem, resulting in Graphjet becoming a wholly-owned subsidiary of Energem. This merger was accounted for as a reverse recapitalization, with Graphjet treated as the acquirer for financial reporting purposes.
  • Organization and Nature of Business: Graphjet Technology is focused on the production of high-quality graphene and graphite, which are critical raw materials used in various industries such as energy storage, electronics, aerospace, and advanced manufacturing.
  • Production Technology: The company utilizes a patented technology that transforms palm kernel shells, a sustainable and renewable agricultural waste product, into artificial graphene and graphite. This process is noted for its lower carbon emissions compared to traditional methods.
  • Research and Development: Graphjet collaborates with the National University of Malaysia and University Teknikal Malaysia Melaka for research and development in graphite and graphene applications, with the Massachusetts Institute of Technology as part of its Industrial Liaison Program.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to sample its graphite and graphene products to multinational companies for market acceptance and procurement purposes, aiming to replace current high-cost suppliers. Starting from June 2025, Graphjet has begun generating revenue from selling side products.
  • Geographical Performance: The company's operations are primarily based in Malaysia, and its functional currency is the Malaysian Ringgit. The company is exposed to foreign exchange risk due to fluctuations between the USD and RM.
  • Segment Reporting: The company operates as a single segment, focusing on the production and development of graphene and graphite products.
  • Lease Commitments: Graphjet has entered into operating lease agreements for its factory in Selangor, Malaysia, with lease terms extending through January 2026.
  • New Equipment Acquisition: On June 26, 2025, Graphjet purchased new equipment valued at approximately $299,000 to enhance production capacity and improve the quality of graphite produced.

SEC Filing: GRAPHJET TECHNOLOGY [ GTI ] - 10-Q - Aug. 15, 2025

data.title: CVS Health Sells About $4 Billion of Notes
data.description:
By Kelly Cloonan

CVS Health has agreed to sell and issue about $4 billion in notes.

The healthcare giant entered into an underwriting agreement with Barclays, J.P. Morgan Securities and Wells Fargo Securities on Aug. 11, according to a filing with the Securities and Exchange Commission on Friday.

Under the agreement, the company said it would sell $750 million in 5% senior notes due in 2032, $1.5 billion in 5.5% senior notes due in 2035, $1.25 billion in 6.2% senior notes due in 2055 and $500 million in 6.3% senior notes due in 2065.

The notes were issued Aug. 15, the company said.

The company expects net proceeds of about $3.96 billion, after deducting the underwriters' discount and estimated offering expenses.

Write to Kelly Cloonan at kelly.cloonan@wsj.com

data.title: Integrity Data Solutions Completes Acquisition of iDoc Corp
data.description:
PHOENIX--(BUSINESS WIRE)--August 15, 2025--

Integrity Data Solutions ("ID Solutions" or the "Company"), through its subsidiary Compu-Call Holdings, LLC ("Compu-Call"), announced that it has acquired iDoc Corp ("iDoc"). The acquisition enhances ID Solutions' ability to deliver a complete range of copy and print solutions, with expanded hardware options, short- and long-term rentals, leasing programs and on-site support.

iDoc is a trusted provider of commercial copier and print hardware, leasing, rental and service throughout Texas. These capabilities will be combined with ID Solutions' comprehensive barcoding and print hardware offerings, managed print services and hardware service solutions, creating a full spectrum offering for customers.

"This acquisition strengthens our position in the print solutions market and expands our hardware and service offerings," said Merrill Matthews, CEO of ID Solutions. "With iDoc's experience and customer base, we can now deliver even more value — whether that's leasing or selling a printer or copier, setting up a rental fleet for a seasonal project, or keeping equipment running at peak performance through our service programs."

"We are excited to become part of ID Solutions," said Bob Buban, Co-Founder and CEO of iDoc Corp. "Our combined resources and expertise mean customers can expect a broader product selection, more flexible and comprehensive solutions, and the same outstanding service they've come to expect."

Integrity Data Solutions is a portfolio company of Falx Capital LLC, a Denver-based private investment company. iDoc represents the third acquisition by ID Solutions since Falx's acquisition of the Company in 2023.

About iDoc Corp:

Founded 1995, iDoc Corp provides commercial copier and printer hardware sales, leasing, rentals, and service, delivering reliable equipment and responsive service to businesses throughout Texas. Learn more at www.idoccorp.com.

About ID Solutions:

ID Solutions is a leading provider of automated data collection, print and supply chain solutions. With a comprehensive portfolio of barcoding and print hardware, maintenance and support services, managed print solutions, and asset tracking software, ID Solutions serves as a trusted partner to customers across North America.
